REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CLAIM FOR REFUND OF INTEREST PAID FAILS TO STATE FACTS TO SUPPORT A CAUSE OF ACTION. UNDER SAN FRANCISCO BUSINESS AND TAX REGULATION CODE 1115.2(B) THE INTEREST ACCRUES ON THE ENTIRE AMOUNT OF THE DELINQUENT TAX, NOT A PART OF IT. TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF ARGUES THAT THIS PROVISION IS CONTRARY TO THE STATE LAW, NO AUTHORITY IS CITED FOR THIS PROPOSITION. CLAIM FOR REFUND-SPECIAL TAX FAILS TO STATE FACTS TO SUPPORT CAUSE OF ACTION. JOHNSON V. COUNTY OF MENDOCINO (2018) 25 CAL.APP.5TH 1017. CLAIM FOR REFUND OF TAXES - COMMERCE CLAUSE FAILS TO STATE FACTS TO SUPPORT A CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF POINTS TO NO ALLEGATIONS IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT SHOWING COMPLIANCE WITH THE GOVERNMENT CLAIMS PROVISIONS. TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF RELIES ON DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE, THE PORTION OF THE CLAIM ATTACHED TO THE RJN AS EXHIBIT 7 QUOTED IN THE OPPOSITION, DOES NOT PROVIDE "SPECIFICITY SUFFICIENT TO ENABLE THE RESPONSIBLE CITY OFFICIALS" TO UNDERSTAND THAT THE CLAIM WAS UNDER THE INTERSTATE COMMERCE CLAUSE AND EVALUATE IT UNDER THE RELEVANT LAW ESPECIALLY IN THE CONTEXT OF THE SECTION OF THE CLAIM IN WHICH THE QUOTED LANGUAGE IS LOCATED. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 5TH, 7TH AND 10TH CAUSES OF ACTION. NON-OPPOSITION FILED. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. SAN FRANCISCO BUSINESS AND TAX REGULATIONS CODE 1114(B). DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE A CLAIM PER TODD SHIPYARDS CORP. V. CITY OF LOS ANGELES (1982) 130 CAL.APP.3D 222, 225-227 AND BALL V. CTY. OF LOS ANGELES (1978) 82 CAL.APP.3D 312, 315, IF PLAINTIFF CAN ALLEGE FACTS GIVING RISE TO A CLAIM FOR INTEREST ON A REFUND. IF PLAINTIFF IS SEEKING A REFUND ON THE INTEREST PAID, SUCH CLAIMS ARE SUBSUMED IN THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, WHICH IS NOT SUBJECT TO THIS DEMURRER. DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. WHETHER THE CITY'S TAX PENALTIES ARE EXCESSIVE AND VIOLATE CONSTITUTIONAL RIGHTS IS AN ISSUE OF FACT THAT CANNOT BE ADDRESSED ON A DEMURRER. THE COURT NOTES HOWEVER, THAT NONE OF THE AUTHORITIES RELIED UPON BY THE PLAINTIFF IN OPPOSITION SUPPORT PLAINTIFF LEGAL POSITION THAT THE PENALTY IS EXCESSIVE. DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT DECLINES PLAINTIFF'S INVITATION TO DISREGARD CIM URBAN REIT 211 MAIN ST. V. CITY & CNTY. OF SAN FRANCISCO (2022) 75 CAL.APP.5TH 939. DEMURRER TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING PLAINTIFF'S ASSERTION THAT THE PROCEEDS OF THE TRANSFER TAX ARE EARMARKED/DEDICATED/REQUIRED TO BE USED FOR A SPECIFIC PROJECT. MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO VACATE STAY

Appearances: Bradley Marsh of Greenberg Traurig, LLP (415.655.1300) for plaintiffs and moving parties; Peter J. Keith of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.4664) for defendants and opposing parties.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The fact that the Board decision may be "final" under the Regulatory Code does not necessarily mean, as the Court required in its order granting the stay, that "the issue of property valuation determination by the Assessment Appeal Board is final." Here, the property valuation has not been finally determined because the Board's decision is currently under judicial review. This Court's prior ruling emphasized "the need for uniformity of administrative regulations," and lifting the stay here creates the risk of conflicting rulings regarding the property's value. Relatedly, Plaintiff's request in the alternative to lift the stay as to cause of action 3 is denied because the property valuation is inseparable from theories of liability in the cause of action. For instance, cause of action 3 alleges that "[t]he Recorder improperly calculated the tax," that "the penalties are imposed at an illegally and unconstitutionally punitive rate of 35% ... [that] bears no relationship to the gravity of the offense," and that "[e]ven if additional tax was due, the penalties should be abated due to reasonable cause as discussed above." (SAC 87, 90, 93). The Court thus cannot lift the stay as to cause of action 3, as it is currently alleged. Order submitted after hearing is under review. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Mr. Marsh contests to the tentative. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO ASSESSOR-RECORDER, SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF SUPERVISORS DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STAY [S.F. LOCAL RULE 8.B.2. - MOTION AFFECTING TRIAL DATE] is GRANTED. This action is stayed until the issue of property valuation determination by the Assessment Appeal Board is final. Such issue should be addressed by the Board due to (1) the need for uniformity of application of administrative regulations and uniformity of answer to administrative questions, and (2) the expert and specialized knowledge of the Board. See Farmers Ins. Exchange v. Superior Ct. (1992) 2 Cal.4th 377; Farr v. County of Nevada (2010) 187 Cal.App.4th 669.

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Colin Fraser 949-732-6500 of Greenberg Traurig, LLP appearing via Zoom for plaintiff.
Peter Keith & Carole Ruwart 415-554-3908 of City Of SF Attorneys Office appearing for defendant.
Reporter: Angela Olvera; CSR# 7265; 661-549-9618; olveracourtreporting@aol.com

Hearing held (See reporter's transcript for full details). Plaintiff contests tentative. Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend.
Claim for Refund of Interest Paid fails to state facts to support a cause of action. Under San Francisco Business and Tax Regulation Code 1115.2(b) the interest accrues on the entire amount of the delinquent tax, not a part of it. To the extent Plaintiff argues that this provision is contrary to the state law, no authority is cited for this proposition.
Claim for Refund-Special Tax fails to state facts to support cause of action. Johnson v. County of Mendocino (2018) 25 Cal.App.5th 1017. Claim for Refund of Taxes - Commerce Clause fails to state facts to support a cause of action. Plaintiff points to no allegations in the Second Amended Complaint showing compliance with the Government Claims provisions. To
the extent Plaintiff relies on Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice, the portion of the claim attached to the RJN as Exhibit 7 quoted in the opposition, does not provide "specificity sufficient to enable the responsible City officials" to understand that the claim was under the interstate commerce clause and evaluate it under the relevant law especially in the context of the section of the Claim in which the quoted language is located.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Young; Reporter: Angela Olvera; CSR# 7265; 661-549-9618; olveracourtreporting@aol.com (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Natassia Kwan and Carole F. Ruwart; Deputy City Attorneys; 415-554-4272; appearing for defendants City and County of San Franciso, San Francisco Assessor-Recorder and San Francisco Board of Supervisors.
Colin Fraser; Greenberg Traurig LLP; 949-732-6500; appearing for plaintiffs Pebblebrook Hotel, LP, Pebblebrook Hotel Trust, Serenity Now, LP, Let It Flho LP, Geary Darling, LP, and SF Treat LP.
Bradley R. Marsh; Greenber Traurig LLP; 415-655-1300; appearing for plaintiffs Pebblebrook Hotel, LP, Pebblebrook Hotel Trust, Serenity Now, LP, Let It Flho LP, Geary Darling, LP, and SF Treat LP.

Plaintiffs and defendants contest the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the 5th, 7th and 10th causes of action. Non-opposition filed.
Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action. San Francisco Business and Tax Regulations Code 1114(b).
Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the third cause of action for Plaintiff to allege a claim per Todd Shipyards Corp. v. City of Los Angeles (1982) 130 Cal.App.3d 222, 225-227 and Ball v. Cty. of Los Angeles (1978) 82 Cal.App.3d 312, 315, if Plaintiff can allege facts giving rise to a claim for interest on a refund. If Plaintiff is seeking a refund on the interest paid, such claims are subsumed in the second cause of action, which is not subject to this demurrer.
Demurrer to the fourth cause of action is overruled. Whether the City's tax penalties are excessive and violate constitutional rights is an issue of fact that cannot be addressed on a demurrer. The Court notes however, that none of the authorities relied upon by the Plaintiff in opposition support Plaintiff legal position that the penalty is excessive.
Demurrer to the sixth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. The Court declines Plaintiff's invitation to disregard CIM Urban REIT 211 Main St. v. City & Cnty. of San Francisco (2022) 75 Cal.App.5th 939.
Demurrer to the eighth cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts supporting Plaintiff's assertion that the proceeds of the transfer tax are earmarked/dedicated/required to be used for a specific project.
Demurrer to the Ninth cause of action is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege the position of the Government Claim putting the City on notice of the violation of the Dormant Commerce Clause and any taxes paid in other jurisdictions.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR #13714, apourtabib@gmail.com, 415-601-7054. (D501)
